item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: 1582.png
Author(s): D Coetzee, Rnbc, faminepulse
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by faminepulse using the images below.

Source: Flickr
License: Public Domain
Title: Closeup on honeypot ants (Myrmecocystus mimicus) at Oakland Zoo
Author: D Coetzee

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Human eye detail, from- Brown human eye (cropped).jpg
Author: Rnbc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was really confusing upon first read, and even then, it's just a human whose parts were divided into an ant colony, with the hook basically being "there is another". Also, the measurement units were wrong, which is pretty bad.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so stupid it's great and I love it.

SCP-1582: Walang, you coward snake! This will not go unpunished!

It appears that the female SCP-1582 colony was in possession of the original SCP-1582's testicles

This is so stupid I laughed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With Faminepulse's approval I made the changes I was thinking of. It's now more authentic!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this article - it's a really interesting premise, and in my home state, too!

I've just PMed the author with some suggestions for ways to make the article more "accurate" to Western Australia. In particular I've given a few suggestions of aboriginal words from the right area. (The words in the article at the moment are from the other side of the country!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first started lurking on this site, one of the first things I thought to look up was "sentient ant colonies." This was amongst the first few results I found. It's a bit weird to say something made me feel nostalgic when I first encountered it less than a year ago, but dammit, this made me feel nostalgic. +1, no hesitation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My, you're right.

Be proud, Faminepulse. When people overlook such common mistake for years and focus on other part, it mean your wirting is truly something.

By the way…

SCP-1582 is roughly 25-75 meters in area…

Is that suppose to be square meters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stopped being able to read it without going "but, but, BUT?" At "3 x 3 x 3" WHAT?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Picture of SCP-1555 gets opened upon clicking the picture in the article. Was that intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this feels so silly, but its so great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great idea, the hook… not so much. +0.5 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it's not clear they're the words in aborigine? (as far as I know) for male genitals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tissues it possesses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this one, simple and sweet.

I suppose it's not important, being an SCP and all, but all ant colonies are female except for the winged reproductive males produced periodically, so his ants would also all be females, or is the other colony "female" because of its humanoid tissues?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to suggest that carbon dating could be more useful, though if it operates like a normal ant colony it would be constantly replacing these elements from surrounding resources. (obviously excluding parts that presumably can't be replaced like the testicles). If the man's DNA has been subject to wear and tear for 300 years it would have to be pretty beat-up; if he's resistant to old age as a result of his formicidation then the parts wouldn't be dateable via DNA.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic - the concept, the picture, the interview… I assumed 'bura' and 'gadya' were just expletives until I looked them up. Was 'Walang' a rival element in 1582's colony or the female colony? It means 'head', so was this the survival instinct turning on the reasoning part of 1582? I was sure when I first read this it said 'he' somewhere in reference to Walang, but I can't find it now. Just one possible flub I noticed, which you may wish to clarify:

SCP-1582 is located in a 3 x 3 x 3 containment cell

Is this a reference to a set of standard proportions (though surely 3x3x3 should just be 'a standard cubic containment cell'), or should there be a unit in there somewhere? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconded, on both counts.

I would buy that the Foundation could determine aparant age by observing the condition of body parts, but why a nose specifically?

DNA testing could tell you that there were body parts form different sources, but not how long ago the swiping occured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I shooped it so I hope it was convincing enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno, this one's really hard to pin down. Thinking about it some more, it seems like he can maybe control some of the ants some of the time…? I'm not really sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That… EFFIN' picture… I saw it and went "Jesus Christ, there's no way the SCP itself will live up to this."
It DID.
Damn you, damn you to hell, and take a +1 with you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn it, you take the core idea of an SCP I'm working on, and manage to up the body horror and make it weirder and more interesting.

Take this +1 and choke on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
estimated to be roughly 300 years old. This data was gathered from a nasal drone instance

How examining piece of the colony tell them that?

Results of DNA testing reveal that the colonies have been warring for body parts since roughly 1790-1800.

How would DNA testing reveal something like that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very very nice. There's room to improve clarity, but that may not be necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this needs a lot of proofreading and a bit of work on tone, but it's damn good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the point at which I utter "Jesus Christ," aloud is the one at which you automatically get my upvote. Excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That interview sealed the deal. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This idea squicked me out in sandbox, and it did the same now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tried to be as straightforward as possible with this one, but I wanted to keep that specific detail ambiguous. I'm against changing it, but if I could get a few more arguments against leaving it vague, - to help me better understand why that's bad - it's going to have to stick.

Thanks for the feedback, greatly appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Ortega: Where is the brain?

SCP-1582: The brain? My brain you mean instead? It needs to be here?

Sounds like the beginning of most press conferences!

Very nicely done. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Really hard to wrap my head around this, but the payoff is fantastic. This is completely horrifying.

I think it would go a long way toward making this immediately understandable if you said the man was "carried" or "in possession of" ants, instead of "made of" them. I mean, the ants don't actually comprise his body, they carry bits of it around. I initially imagined, well, a dude made out of ants.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gosh, this is just fun. It feels like the best kind of myth - the sort where Zeus is raping shepherd-boys, and Coyote is stealing the moon, and (apparently) people can be ant-colonies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this version much better. (I liked the old one too, just not its redactions)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad that my initial apprehension at yet another ant-based SCP was dispelled. You did a good job on this, and it's delightfully weird.

Also, for whatever reason I feel for poor Dr. Ortega. Dude has to sit around waiting for the guy made of ants to start the interview, and then he gets a bunch of gibberish and the ants go away. That would make for an amusing diary entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not based on myth, more based on honey pot ants and working off the image. And thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this idea, and I liked it when it was first posted. It's weird and perfect, and I'm sure it's based on some weird myth I don't know about, but it even works without the knowledge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP and I love it's name too

ANT MAN
